beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: The warren opens up. Packed earth, low-slung structures built close together against the cold. The smell of coal smoke and damp wool. People who know exactly how much space they occupy.

beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: The canyon widens at its far end. The wind off the mountain is different here — clean and cold, coming from somewhere open.

opening_beats.speaker: 0001_TOWN
opening_beats.text: You're new. You're with Sidhal, I think, from the way you're walking. This is The Grimes. Don't lose anything you can't afford to lose.

responses.text: What should I know about this place?

beats.speaker: 0001_TOWN
beats.text: Everyone here knows everyone else's business. Not because they're nosy — because they have to. It's how you stay safe. If someone asks you something personal, they're probably not being rude.

opening_beats.speaker: 0005_TOWN
opening_beats.text: You from the castle? I can tell by the letter in your pocket. Castle lottery. First time I've seen one of them come through Aetherfield on foot.

responses.text: What's the aqueduct situation like here?

beats.speaker: 0005_TOWN
beats.text: Three breaks in the last six weeks. Same section every time. The stone there is old — pre-construction. Nobody's authorised to replace it because it's technically under temple jurisdiction. So we patch it and wait.

opening_beats.speaker: 0006_TOWN
opening_beats.text: James told me about you. The lottery apprentice. We don't get many of those down here. Usually they find someone who already lives near the castle.

responses.text: How long have you been working the aqueduct?

beats.speaker: 0006_TOWN
beats.text: Nine years. I know every section of it. I know which parts the foreman reports accurately and which parts he rounds down on the damage assessments. Both kinds matter to know.

opening_beats.speaker: 0011_TOWN
opening_beats.text: You're not here to buy candles. The candles are for the people who come here to buy candles. You look like you want something else. That's fine. I have something else.

beats.speaker: 0011_TOWN
beats.text: The kind that moves when the market doesn't have space for it. Materials, mostly. Information sometimes. If you need access to either, you come back when you know exactly what you need. I don't do browsing.

beats.speaker: 0011_TOWN
beats.text: Nobody just looks in here. Come back when you need something real.

opening_beats.speaker: 0002_TOWN
opening_beats.text: Sidhal mentioned you were coming through. Lottery apprentice. I've been foreman here for eleven years and I've never seen one of you come this way before. That's interesting.

beats.speaker: 0002_TOWN
beats.text: Most apprentices arrive at the castle from the north road. Someone who comes through Serpent's Pass either doesn't know the direct route or chose not to take it. I'm assuming Sidhal made the choice for you. He doesn't do that without a reason.

opening_beats.speaker: 0003_TOWN
opening_beats.text: You've been through The Grimes. I can tell. You have that look people get when they see things they're not sure what to do with yet.

responses.text: It was a lot to take in.

beats.speaker: 0003_TOWN
beats.text: That is the correct response. The people who take it in quickly either already knew or didn't really see it. You'll do better than both of those.
